channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Lenovo Legion Go S is RAMageddon’s latest victim
item.description: You can still find the Asus Xbox Ally X and the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus for $999 and $1,049 respectively, but Lenovo's Legion Go S has seemingly given up the fight. The best version of Lenovo's 8-inch handheld now costs nearly double what it did at launch - originally $829.99 last summer, the SteamOS […]

item.title: “Essentially invisible:” How hackers 'trojan-horsed' QEMU virtual machines to bypass security and drop ransomware
item.description: Hackers hide ransomware tools inside virtual machines using QEMU, allowing attacks to bypass security controls and remain largely invisible.

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
item.description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

item.title: "100x faster than traditional tools": Scientists load quantum computer with first complete genome to crack biology’s 'impossible' puzzle — in time for World Quantum Day
item.description: Scientists load a complete genome into a quantum computer, moving faster DNA analysis closer to reality.

item.title: Veremark Background Check review: A surprisingly practical solution for businesses of all sizes
item.description: Veremark offers a global background screening platform that streamlines hiring through automation, secure data handling, and customizable workflows.

item.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in seven new countries
item.description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam.

item.title: Google brings Gemini in Chrome to users in Australia, Japan, Singapore and South Korea
item.description: After debuting in the US, Gemini in Chrome is making its way to more markets. Starting today, Google is rolling out Chrome's built-in chatbot to users in countries in East Asia and the Pacific, including Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Vietnam. The expansion comes after Google earlier this year made Gemini in Chrome available to people in Canada, India and New Zealand. With the exception of Japan, where Google isn't making the new suite available on iOS just yet, everyone else in the countries mentioned above can access Gemini in Chrome through Chrome's...

item.title: John Ternus is taking over from Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
item.description: The multitrillion-dollar home of the iPhone, Mac, and so many other tech gadgets is getting a new leader this fall, as Apple has announced that Tim Cook will be replaced as CEO on September 1st by current hardware boss John Ternus. How will we look back on Cook’s legacy, and what will Apple look like […]

item.title: Read Tim Cook’s letter to the Apple world as he departs as CEO
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has written a letter to the community as he prepares to step down from his role in September. "This is not goodbye. But at this moment of transition, I wanted to take the opportunity to say thank you," Cook writes. As part of the shift, John Ternus, Apple's senior vice president […]

item.title: Best Kitchen Composters and Food Recyclers (2026)
item.description: Responsibly dispose of your food scraps with one of these indoor, (mostly) odor-free, WIRED-tested devices.

item.title: "Relying on a single, centralized mine represents a risk": US wants rare earth independence from China — but can DARPA actually make it work?
item.description: US turns to DARPA’s Smash program to fix rare earth processing bottlenecks instead of opening new mines.

item.title: Tim Cook quittera la présidence d’Apple en septembre prochain après 15 ans de règne
item.description: Coup de tonnerre chez Apple ! Après quinze ans au poste de CEO, Tim Cook cède sa place au profit de John Ternus. Le changement sera effectif en septembre prochain après quelques semaines de transition.

item.title: John Ternus named new CEO of Apple, will replace Tim Cook in September
item.description: Ternus, Apple’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will be the new chief executive as Tim Cook becomes executive chairman. John Ternus, Apple’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, has been named the new CEO of Apple, the company announced.

item.title: Tim Cook stepping down at Apple, John Ternus will become new CEO
item.description: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO in September, and John Ternus will assume the role, while Cook remains as Executive Chairman.

item.title: C’est officiel, Tim Cook quitte la direction d’Apple
item.description: Tim Cook cédera sa place à la tête d'Apple en septembre. C'est John Ternus qui le remplacera dès le lancement du prochain iPhone 18.

item.title: Apple names Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer
item.description: Apple has appointed Johny Srouji as its new chief hardware officer, "effective immediately," according to an announcement on Monday. He is stepping into the shoes of current hardware engineering head John Ternus, who will replace Tim Cook as CEO in September, while Cook becomes the chairman of the board. Last December, Srouji reassured his team […]

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down
item.description: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

item.title: I turned my text messages into a song with Suno and now my dinner plans sound like a pop duet
item.description: Tricks to avoid sounding like an AI chatbot

item.title: John Ternus will be CEO of Apple when Tim Cook steps down this fall
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook is officially stepping down from his role on September 1, the company announced today, while current SVP of hardware engineering John Ternus will take over as the new CEO. Cook will transition to a new role as executive chairman of Apple’s Board of Directors. The company says the move was “approved unanimously” by Apple’s Board, and that Cook will work on transitioning his duties over the summer. “It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company,” Cook said in a statement. “I love Apple with a...

item.title: Ben McKenzie Says Crypto Has a Secret Ingredient: Male Loneliness
item.description: The actor-director discussed his least favorite currency and read a series of mean tweets—about us!—at our inaugural WIRED@Night event.

item.title: 'The 1000 TOPS consumer': How the NPU-everywhere strategy could turn every user into a walking supercomputer by 2030
item.description: The 1,000 TOPS consumer: Multiple devices could deliver powerful distributed AI capability by 2030
